objective: Remediate the protocol-v4 cryptographic, persistence, membership, relay-causality, application-state, and build-boundary findings with repository-grounded controls and authoritative regression evidence.
source: User directive: keep going until all security holes are patched
authoritativeCompletionProof: The complete npm test security and product matrices, focused protocol-v4 browser and relay regressions, pinned OpenMLS/Rust checks, dependency audits, typecheck, production build, UI browser suites, and Wrangler dry-run all pass against the reconciled working tree.
behavioralOracle: Protected room/game content remains inside authenticated MLS application messages; membership, causal order, replay, crash recovery, and terminal teardown fail closed under malformed, stale, reordered, duplicated, disconnected, or barrier-conflicting traffic while valid multi-member gameplay remains usable.

globalStopConditions: a required cryptographic primitive has no production-suitable browser implementation
the only apparent repair would silently weaken an existing protection
unrelated user changes overlap a required security edit and intent cannot be safely preserved

tasks.title: Implement and pin the protocol-v4 MLS cryptographic boundary
tasks.summary: Replace static shared room encryption with strict protocol-v4 per-device MLS identities, bounded wire contracts, audited WASM adapters, and reproducibly pinned browser artifacts.

coordinationHazards.description: Protocol consumers and generated browser artifacts could drift from the reviewed MLS source contract.
coordinationHazards.mitigation: Use strict shared parsers plus source-input and browser-artifact hash verification before build.
coordinationHazards.verification: Protocol/security tests, pinned Rust checks, and the OpenMLS artifact verifier pass.

acceptance.property: Room cryptography uses standards-based per-device MLS epochs with Add, Remove, Update, forward secrecy after erasure, and post-compromise recovery after fresh honest entropy.
acceptance.oracle: OpenMLS protocol/browser regressions and pinned Rust tests exercise create, join, membership changes, update, application encryption, restore, and obsolete-state rejection.

acceptance.property: Protocol v4 rejects malformed, oversized, non-canonical, cross-room, cross-suite, unknown-field, and downgrade traffic, and build artifacts match reviewed inputs.
acceptance.oracle: The complete security suite and artifact verifier pass before the production build.

changedSurfaces.name: OpenMLS browser/WASM and pinned artifact boundary

initialProbe.expectedResult: The earlier protocol exposes a deterministic shared room key and lacks per-device epochs.
initialProbe.observedResult: Confirmed before remediation: the legacy path had one shared symmetric room key and no authenticated membership epochs.
initialProbe.rationale: This establishes the original forward-secrecy and membership defect without relying on the replacement implementation.

environment.runtime: Bun, Node.js, Chromium, and browser-compatible WebCrypto

verification.rollback: Test fixtures and browser profiles are isolated and disposable.
verification.rationale: The authoritative repository gate includes the isolated protocol-v4 security suites.
Verifies four pinned browser artifacts against 27 reviewed source inputs.

verification.command: cargo +1.94.1 fmt --all -- --check && cargo +1.94.1 test --locked && cargo +1.94.1 clippy --locked --all-targets -- -D warnings
verification.replay: cargo +1.94.1 fmt --all -- --check && cargo +1.94.1 test --locked && cargo +1.94.1 clippy --locked --all-targets -- -D warnings
verification.rationale: Checks the pinned Rust implementation, locked dependency graph, and warning-free source.

stopConditions: a production-suitable browser MLS implementation cannot satisfy the strict contract

tasks.title: Make cryptographic and replay state durable and single-writer
tasks.summary: Persist one authenticated, encrypted, revisioned room/device state in IndexedDB under a live Web Lock, with CAS writes, rollback state, outbox state, and durable replay tombstones.

coordinationHazards.description: Concurrent tabs or a crash between cryptographic mutation and network output could fork state or reuse causal material.
coordinationHazards.mitigation: Require a live per-room Web Lock and a successful CAS of the complete wrapped state before returning wire output.
coordinationHazards.verification: Restart, takeover, CAS-failure, lock-loss, replay, and mutation-boundary regressions pass.

deliverables: persistent replay and crash recovery tests

acceptance.property: Accepted relay and application identifiers remain rejected after reload, reconnect, and local room-state erasure where a tombstone is required.
acceptance.oracle: Persistent replay tests restore state and submit exact stale identifiers/ciphertexts, observing fail-closed rejection.

acceptance.property: Only one tab can mutate a device state and any persistence, revision, or lease failure prevents dependent wire output.
acceptance.oracle: Two-tab takeover and injected persistence/lock-loss tests prove one writer and restoration or terminal failure before send.

changedSurfaces.name: wrapped IndexedDB state and persistent replay ledger
cross-tab ownership, takeover, and terminal lock-loss handling

initialProbe.expectedResult: Legacy replay authority is tab-scoped and disappears with its session.
initialProbe.observedResult: Confirmed before remediation: replay state was held in sessionStorage and could not provide durable cross-tab or restart authority.
initialProbe.rationale: Source inspection directly established the persistence gap.

verification.rollback: Tests use isolated IndexedDB databases and browser contexts.
verification.rationale: Authoritative security gate includes seven persistent replay tests and browser crash/lease paths.

stopConditions: required browser persistence or locking APIs are unavailable in a supported target

tasks.title: Enforce authenticated membership and durable relay causality
tasks.summary: Bind invitation-authorized device credentials and KeyPackages to explicit host admission; enforce membership barriers, durable causal grants/results, exact resume, bounded rate classes, and persisted terminal teardown in both relay runtimes.

out: availability against a malicious relay or host

owns: sender result and terminal delivery ordering

coordinationHazards.description: Lost results, reconnect races, or membership epoch changes can deadlock or fork client and relay causal state.
coordinationHazards.mitigation: Persist exact grants, results, backlog prefixes, barriers, cancellation reasons, snapshots, and terminal state before dependent effects.
coordinationHazards.verification: Adversarial relay/controller/runtime tests cover duplicate, stale, reordered, disconnected, close-race, barrier, and terminal paths.

acceptance.property: Only an invitation-bound per-device credential and fresh KeyPackage can be explicitly admitted, and active resume cannot introduce new membership key material.
acceptance.oracle: Admission, substitution, challenge, deduplication, activation, and resume regressions reject rebinding and automatic admission.

acceptance.property: Relay grants, sender results, backlog replay, membership barriers, and lifecycle transitions remain exact and durable across rejection, disconnect, restart, and resume.
acceptance.oracle: Relay and controller suites prove persist-before-effect/ACK, exact prefix replay, authoritative snapshot reconciliation, FIFO removal provenance, and barrier exclusivity.

acceptance.property: Mandatory protocol fanout remains live within bounded abuse budgets; blocked immediate order requests cannot wedge client intent; socket close cannot discard an already-received terminal frame; and terminal retirement is persisted before exact-member notification.
acceptance.oracle: Local/DO rate and terminal tests, exact durable cancellation replay, queued-close controller coverage, and rapid four-member browser banter all pass without spurious rate-limit disconnects.

changedSurfaces.name: durable protocol-v4 relay and causal membership state machine
local and Durable Object WebSocket runtimes

initialProbe.expectedResult: Pre-repair tests expose admission rebinding, replay/order, reconnect, barrier, and terminal lifecycle gaps.
initialProbe.observedResult: The security audit reproduced those state-machine failures before implementing protocol-v4 controls and adversarial regressions.
initialProbe.rationale: Membership and distributed causal safety require behavioral state-machine oracles, not source shape alone.

verification.rollback: Tests use disposable relay and browser state.
verification.rationale: Exercises 48 exact controller/relay cases with 458 assertions.

environment.runtime: Bun with local and Durable Object runtime harnesses

verification.rollback: Runtime harness state is in-memory and disposable.
verification.rationale: 31 runtime tests and 630 assertions cover split budgets, terminal notification, authentication, and durable runtime transitions.

verification.command: env PILLOWFORT_COMPARE_SNAPSHOTS=0 bun test --timeout 120000 --max-concurrency 1 -t 'banter flows' test/game-night.test.ts
verification.rollback: Browser rooms and profiles are disposable.
verification.replay: env PILLOWFORT_COMPARE_SNAPSHOTS=0 bun test --timeout 120000 --max-concurrency 1 -t 'banter flows' test/game-night.test.ts
verification.rationale: A real four-member room completes three admissions and nine rapid application events without spurious rate limiting or socket closure.

stopConditions: the only repair would allow relay state to override authenticated MLS or signed application authority

tasks.title: Move room and game semantics inside the authenticated E2EE log
tasks.summary: Represent profiles, presence, chat, drawing, room controls, and every game as strict device-signed MLS application events reduced deterministically with bounded snapshots and history redaction.

coordinationHazards.description: Legacy direct WebSocket actions or nondeterministic clients could bypass signed causal state and diverge game outcomes.
coordinationHazards.mitigation: Map every protected action to one strict signed event and apply it through a shared deterministic reducer.
coordinationHazards.verification: Application, reducer, game, integration, full-tour, mobile, quad-view, and game-night suites pass.

acceptance.property: All protected room and game semantics are authenticated inside MLS and reject forgery, replay, sequence/order regression, non-members, malformed content, and invalid reducer transitions.
acceptance.oracle: Application-event and reducer suites cover every event family, signature/order failures, snapshots, and game preconditions.

acceptance.property: Valid chat, drawing, RPS, tic-tac-toe, Saboteur, King of the Hill, pillow fight, host transfer, and terminal flows remain usable across desktop and mobile multiplayer UI.
acceptance.oracle: Game/unit/integration plus deterministic UI, full-tour, quad-view, mobile, design-snapshot, and four-member game-night browser suites pass.

changedSurfaces.name: signed application-event and deterministic reducer contract
secure room and multiplayer game UI

initialProbe.expectedResult: Before remediation, no single signed and bounded application-event contract covers every feature.
initialProbe.observedResult: The audit confirmed protected semantics escaped the prior chat-only encryption boundary.
initialProbe.rationale: A complete event inventory was necessary to prevent security-by-feature omission.

verification.rollback: All game and browser fixtures are disposable.
verification.rationale: Authoritative gate passed 239 base tests/1004 assertions plus 18 isolated security files, including application, reducer, game, and integration coverage.

verification.rollback: Visual outputs are versioned deterministic baselines.
verification.rationale: 36 UI tests and 154 assertions pass across mobile, quad, demo, full-tour, and game-night journeys.

verification.rollback: Snapshot outputs are versioned deterministic baselines.
verification.rationale: Five design snapshot tests and ten assertions pass.

stopConditions: a protected product action cannot be represented deterministically without a product decision

tasks.title: Reconcile security claims and complete the release verification boundary
tasks.summary: Audit changed surfaces and dependencies, document fixed findings and residual limits without overclaiming, and prove the tree typechecks, builds, and packages for Cloudflare without performing deployment.

coordinationHazards.description: A green focused suite could hide integration, dependency, build, or documentation drift, and a dry-run could be mistaken for deployment.
coordinationHazards.mitigation: Require the broad product/security matrix, offline dependency evidence, typecheck/build, line-reference audit, Wrangler dry-run, and an explicit no-deployment scope statement.
coordinationHazards.verification: All local release gates pass and versioned artifacts make no deployment or external synchronization claim.

deliverables: schema-valid immutable attempt reports and status

acceptance.property: Security documentation accurately maps each fixed finding class and late adversarial subcase to existing source/tests while retaining material architectural and operational limits.
acceptance.oracle: Every backticked file/range exists, report claims match the final controls, residual risks remain explicit, and no deployment is claimed.

acceptance.property: The complete tree passes authoritative tests, typecheck, pinned cryptographic checks, dependency review, production build, marketing checks, and Cloudflare packaging dry-run.
acceptance.oracle: All commands recorded in the final attempt report return zero against the same working tree; offline audits are described with their actual cache limitations.

changedSurfaces.name: security claims, residual limits, and source/test traceability
production build and Cloudflare package boundary
changedSurfaces.location: package.json, client build, wrangler.toml, and marketing

initialProbe.command: git status --short && rg -n "Remaining boundary|Residual risk" security_best_practices_report.md docs/SECURE_ROOM_PROTOCOL_V4.md
initialProbe.expectedResult: The broad dirty-tree scope and documented limitations require reconciliation before a release claim.
initialProbe.observedResult: The working tree contains integrated product/security changes; stale manifest owners/paths and late security subcases required final evidence reconciliation.
initialProbe.rationale: Release proof must describe the exact integrated tree, not an earlier task plan.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.replay: npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.rationale: Typecheck passes; build verifies OpenMLS inputs and emits the production client bundle with only the standard size warning.

verification.rollback: Remove the temporary dry-run output directory.
verification.rationale: Packages 8 assets and the ROOM Durable Object/ASSETS bindings without publishing or changing Cloudflare state.

verification.replay: Run npm audit --offline --omit=dev separately in ., client, and marketing
verification.rationale: All three production dependency trees report zero vulnerabilities from the available offline npm advisory cache.
Scans 136 locked dependencies with no vulnerability advisory; the report retains the allowed unmaintained instant 0.1.13 warning and the no-fetch/index limitations.

verification.replay: cd marketing && npm run lint && npm test
verification.rationale: Marketing build and all three rendered HTML/CMS tests pass.

verification.command: node -e <validate backticked source/test paths and line ranges> && git diff --check
verification.replay: Re-run the documented path/range checker and git diff --check
verification.rationale: All referenced paths/ranges exist and the working-tree diff has no whitespace errors.

requiresApproval: commit, push, and production deployment are performed by the release owner outside this evidence task
stopConditions: a required authoritative release gate remains failing
